sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Reports left back pain and hematuria.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a history of kidney stones, with this episode being his third. He states the pain started approximately three days ago, initially on his left back, and has now traveled lower, but he hasn't passed the stone yet. He describes the pain as constant and states he cannot get comfortable. He reports seeing blood in his urine this morning. He has tried taking Tylenol and ibuprofen, but finds them ineffective. He previously experienced kidney stone episodes approximately three to four days after passing them. He notes some nausea and chills, and reports being in significant pain, making it difficult to eat. He has been taking Tylenol and ibuprofen, but finds them ineffective. He has not been consistent with taking his blood pressure readings, with readings ranging from one thirty to one fifty and a bottom number between seventy and eighty.
Findings
The physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation in the abdomen and cVA tinnitus on the right flank. The patient's blood pressure readings were sometimes in the one forties. A grade two systolic murmur was heard on heart exam. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally. The patient denies chest pain and reports no abdominal pain.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient presents with a likely kidney stone, complicated by inflammation of the kidney. He also has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
Plan
Prescribe oxycodone 5 mg every 6-8 hours as needed for pain. Provide a strainer for urine to monitor stone passage. Refer to urology for further evaluation and management. Schedule a follow-up in four weeks if symptoms persist. Provide a referral to a registered dietitian to assist with dietary modifications, particularly regarding salt intake. Continue metformin as prescribed. Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust medications as needed.
